item.title: backlog edit --depends-on replaces entire dependsOn array instead of appending like --step and --ac
item.intent: Ensure --depends-on on backlog edit is consistent with --step and --ac: multiple calls should append dependencies, not replace the entire list each time.
item.description: In cmd_backlog_edit(), the --depends-on flag builds a deps_json array from the provided value and uses '.dependsOn = $deps' which replaces the entire dependsOn array. By contrast, --step uses '.steps = ((.steps // []) + $newsteps)' and --ac uses '.acceptanceCriteria = ((.acceptanceCriteria // []) + $newac)' — both append. This inconsistency means that if you add a dependency incrementally (e.g., two separate calls to add two dependencies), the second call wipes out the first. A user who wants to add one dependency at a time must always specify the full list. The fix should make --depends-on append to the existing dependsOn array, matching the behavior of --step and --ac. A separate --clear-depends-on flag should be added to allow full replacement when needed.
steps: In cmd_backlog_edit(), find the --depends-on case that builds deps_json and sets .dependsOn = $deps
Change the jq expression from '.dependsOn = $deps' to '.dependsOn = ((.dependsOn // []) + $deps) | unique'
Add a --clear-depends-on flag that sets '.dependsOn = []', consistent with --clear-steps and --clear-scope
Update the usage/help string in cmd_backlog_edit to mention --clear-depends-on
Test: run backlog edit ID --depends-on A, then backlog edit ID --depends-on B, and verify both A and B appear in dependsOn
acceptanceCriteria: Running 'backlog edit ID --depends-on A' followed by 'backlog edit ID --depends-on B' results in dependsOn=[A, B]
Running 'backlog edit ID --depends-on A,B' in a single call also works correctly
Running 'backlog edit ID --clear-depends-on' clears the dependsOn array to []
No regression: existing --depends-on behavior for first-time setting still works
